title: Sophia — the Wisdom Gate: a provenance-aware, verifier-gated reasoning layer that abstains instead of fabricating
description: Sophia is a provenance-aware, verifier-gated reasoning layer for large language models that abstains instead of fabricating. Given a claim, it verifies the claim against machine-readable sources and then accepts, abstains, or blocks. The system targets attribution hallucination and is evaluated under a pre-registered no-overclaim measurement gate (≥2 independent judge families, reported inter-judge agreement, ≥3 runs, and confidence intervals). This release (0.11.0) adds a layered, independence-labelled independent-verification toolkit: viral-claim fact-check (Google ClaimReview), authorship and wrong-creator attribution checks (Wikidata), fabricated-citation existence (Crossref, the Mata v. Avianca mode), and a multi-judge source-faithfulness check for misstated findings — each verdict tagged with its independence tier, fail-open on ignorance, with a self-contained third-party replication pack. An honest finding is recorded as part of the method: catching open-world contamination requires an oracle that covers the claim, fail-closed strictness, or model knowledge — there is no free lunch — and one earlier over-block figure was found to be a stale-report artifact and withdrawn. Archiving this release on Zenodo mints a permanent DOI and a fixed authorship + date record as part of the project's defensive-publication / provenance strategy. canClaimAGI remains false.
